objective: Implement a new utility function as a candidate change. This is a CODE-WRITING task, not an inspection — produce the complete implementation, do not report 'no change'. Create the function `truncateByGraphemes` intended for a new file `src/util/truncate-by-graphemes.ts`. Signature: `export function truncateByGraphemes(input: string, maxLength: number): string`. Behaviour: return the first `maxLength` user-perceived characters of `input`; if `input` was longer than `maxLength` user-perceived characters, append a single ellipsis character '…' (U+2026) to the returned prefix. The function MUST NOT split a multi-code-unit character (an emoji, a surrogate pair, or a base character plus combining marks / ZWJ sequence) across the truncation boundary. Provide the full function body.

requirements.text: Implement truncateByGraphemes(input, maxLength) that truncates by user-perceived characters (graphemes), never splitting a surrogate pair, emoji, or combining/ZWJ sequence, and appends '…' only when the input was actually truncated.
acceptanceCriteria: truncateByGraphemes('hello', 10) returns 'hello' unchanged (shorter than maxLength, no ellipsis appended).
A single emoji such as '😀' counts as ONE user-perceived character, not two UTF-16 code units.
truncateByGraphemes('👩‍👩‍👧 family', 1) must not return a broken half of the ZWJ emoji sequence.
maxLength <= 0 is handled without throwing and returns a consistent result (empty string or a lone ellipsis).
The candidate is produced without writing to git and without modifying any tracked file (candidate-only).
